description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow named CI that runs on push events. The workflow has two jobs: 'activate' and 'build'. The 'activate' job runs on a self-hosted ubuntu-20.04 environment, checks specific conditions in the commit message, and simply outputs 'ok go'. The 'build' job depends on 'activate', runs in a containerized ubuntu-20.04 environment, checks out the repository with submodules, logs in to the GitHub Container Registry, configures and builds the Feel++ project using cmake, tests it with ctest, downloads artifacts, sets up QEMU and Docker Buildx, and logs in to the registry again.
